safest_plan.entry_basis: No fresh entry. The only cited level was a member's livestream level (Studentoffew) that has already held and run ~+40% — entering now is chasing, not a clean setup.
safest_plan.stop_basis: The source ran a break-even stop after the move; no structural invalidation level was given for a new entry, so none can be cited.

stance_by_source.summary: A member's trade (not a Hub call) off a livestream level that held and is now up about 40%; flagged as questionable on Sharia grounds because it's telehealth.

plain_english: Only one mention here, and it's a member's own trade — not an official call — that already worked: it bought a level that held and is now up about 40%. There's no fresh entry, no stated targets, and no clear level to risk against, so buying now would just be chasing a move that already happened. On top of that the business needs a Sharia check before you'd own it. Wait — let it come back to a defined level and pull up real chart structure before doing anything.
